req.title: Bill pay (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# pytest Tests for Bill Payment Requirements

```python
"""
Test Suite: Bill Payment - Registered Biller
Requirement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000
             with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.
             Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, time
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service interfaces assumed to exist in production code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from payments.services.bill_payment import BillPaymentService
from payments.models import (
    Account,
    BillPayment,
    Biller,
    Customer,
    PaymentResult,
    PaymentStatus,
    SettlementDate,
)
from payments.exceptions import (
    BillerNotRegisteredError,
    CustomerNotAuthorisedError,
    DailyLimitExceededError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    InvalidPaymentAmountError,
    PaymentLimitExceededError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MIN_PAYMENT = Decimal("1.00")
MAX_PAYMENT = Decimal("50000.00")
SYDNEY_TZ = ZoneInfo("Australia/Sydney")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def registered_biller() -> Biller:
    """A fully registered biller with a 3 PM AEST cut-off."""
    return Biller(
        biller_id="BILLER-001",
        name="City Water Authority",
        is_registered=True,
        cut_off_time=time(15, 0),          # 15:00 local
        cut_off_timezone=SYDNEY_TZ,
        settlement_lead_days=1,             # next business day after cut-off
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def authorised_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-001",
        name="Jane Doe",
        is_active=True,
        is_authorised_for_bill_pay=True,
        daily_payment_limit=Decimal("100000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def unauthorised_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-002",
        name="John Blocked",
        is_active=True,
        is_authorised_for_bill_pay=False,
        daily_payment_limit=Decimal("100000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def funded_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-001",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
        available_balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def low_balance_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-002",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("0.50"),
        available_balance=Decimal("0.50"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def zero_balance_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-003",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        available_balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def bill_payment_service(
    registered_biller: Biller,
    funded_account: Account,
) -> BillPaymentService:
    """Real service wired with mock repository/gateway collaborators."""
    payment_repo = MagicMock()
    account_repo = MagicMock()
    biller_registry = MagicMock()
    calendar_service = MagicMock()

    biller_registry.get.return_value = registered_biller
    account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account

    return BillPaymentService(
        payment_repo=payment_repo,
        account_repo=account_repo,
        biller_registry=biller_registry,
        calendar_service=calendar_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def make_payment(
    service: BillPaymentService,
    *,
    biller_id: str = "BILLER-001",
    account_id: str = "ACC-001",
    amount: Decimal = Decimal("100.00"),
    reference: str = "INV-2024-001",
    initiated_at: datetime | None = None,
) -> PaymentResult:
    initiated_at = initiated_at or datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
    return service.pay(
        biller_id=biller_id,
        account_id=account_id,
        amount=amount,
        reference=reference,
        initiated_at=initiated_at,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 1 – Happy-path: valid amounts
# ===========================================================================


class TestValidPaymentAmounts:
    """Payments within [$1, $50,000] must be accepted."""

    def test_minimum_amount_accepted(self, bill_payment_service):
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=MIN_PAYMENT)
        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED

    def test_maximum_amount_accepted(self, bill_payment_service):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value.available_balance = Decimal(
            "50000.00"
        )
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=MAX_PAYMENT)
        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "amount",
        [
            Decimal("1.00"),
            Decimal("1.01"),
            Decimal("500.00"),
            Decimal("9999.99"),
            Decimal("25000.00"),
            Decimal("49999.99"),
            Decimal("50000.00"),
        ],
    )
    def test_amounts_within_range_accepted(self, bill_payment_service, amount):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value.available_balance = Decimal(
            "50000.00"
        )
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=amount)
        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED

    def test_accepted_result_contains_payment_id(self, bill_payment_service):
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("250.00"))
        assert result.payment_id is not None
        assert len(result.payment_id) > 0

    def test_accepted_result_contains_correct_amount(self, bill_payment_service):
        amount = Decimal("123.45")
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=amount)
        assert result.amount == amount

    def test_accepted_result_contains_biller_id(self, bill_payment_service):
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))
        assert result.biller_id == "BILLER-001"


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 2 – Negative path: amount out of range
# ===========================================================================


class TestInvalidPaymentAmounts:
    """Payments outside [$1, $50,000] must be rejected with InvalidPaymentAmountError."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "amount",
        [
            Decimal("0.00"),
            Decimal("0.99"),
            Decimal("-0.01"),
            Decimal("-1.00"),
            Decimal("-9999.00"),
        ],
    )
    def test_amount_below_minimum_raises(self, bill_payment_service, amount):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidPaymentAmountError) as exc_info:
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=amount)
        assert "minimum" in str(exc_info.value).lower()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "amount",
        [
            Decimal("50000.01"),
            Decimal("50001.00"),
            Decimal("100000.00"),
            Decimal("999999.99"),
        ],
    )
    def test_amount_above_maximum_raises(self, bill_payment_service, amount):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidPaymentAmountError, PaymentLimitExceededError)):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=amount)

    def test_zero_amount_raises(self, bill_payment_service):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidPaymentAmountError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("0.00"))

    def test_fractional_cents_raises(self, bill_payment_service):
        """Three decimal places is not a valid currency amount."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidPaymentAmountError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("10.001"))


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 3 – Negative path: insufficient funds
# ===========================================================================


class TestInsufficientFunds:
    """Payment must fail when available balance < requested amount."""

    def test_payment_exceeds_available_balance_raises(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        low_balance_account,
    ):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = low_balance_account
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError) as exc_info:
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("1.00"))
        assert exc_info.value.available_balance == Decimal("0.50")
        assert exc_info.value.requested_amount == Decimal("1.00")

    def test_zero_balance_raises(self, bill_payment_service, zero_balance_account):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = zero_balance_account
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=MIN_PAYMENT)

    def test_exact_balance_match_is_accepted(self, bill_payment_service, funded_account):
        """Edge case: available_balance == amount should succeed."""
        funded_account.available_balance = Decimal("100.00")
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))
        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED

    def test_insufficient_funds_error_message_is_informative(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        low_balance_account,
    ):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = low_balance_account
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError) as exc_info:
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("50.00"))
        assert exc_info.value.shortfall == Decimal("49.50")

    def test_no_funds_debited_on_insufficient_funds(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        low_balance_account,
    ):
        original_balance = low_balance_account.available_balance
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = low_balance_account
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("1.00"))
        assert low_balance_account.available_balance == original_balance


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 4 – Negative path: unauthorised customer
# ===========================================================================


class TestUnauthorisedCustomer:
    """Payments must be rejected for customers not authorised for bill pay."""

    @pytest.fixture()
    def account_for_unauthorised(
        self, unauthorised_customer: Customer
    ) -> Account:
        return Account(
            account_id="ACC-UNAUTH",
            owner=unauthorised_customer,
            balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
            available_balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
            currency="AUD",
        )

    def test_unauthorised_customer_raises(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        account_for_unauthorised,
    ):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = account_for_unauthorised
        with pytest.raises(CustomerNotAuthorisedError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))

    def test_unauthorised_error_exposes_customer_id(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        account_for_unauthorised,
    ):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = account_for_unauthorised
        with pytest.raises(CustomerNotAuthorisedError) as exc_info:
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))
        assert exc_info.value.customer_id == "CUST-002"

    def test_inactive_customer_raises(self, bill_payment_service, funded_account):
        funded_account.owner.is_active = False
        with pytest.raises(CustomerNotAuthorisedError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))

    def test_unregistered_biller_raises(self, bill_payment_service, registered_biller):
        registered_biller.is_registered = False
        bill_payment_service._biller_registry.get.return_value = registered_biller
        with pytest.raises(BillerNotRegisteredError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))

    def test_unknown_biller_id_raises(self, bill_payment_service):
        bill_payment_service._biller_registry.get.side_effect = BillerNotRegisteredError(
            biller_id="UNKNOWN-99"
        )
        with pytest.raises(BillerNotRegisteredError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, biller_id="UNKNOWN-99")


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 5 – Negative path: daily / transaction limit exceeded
# ===========================================================================


class TestLimitsExceeded:
    """Payments must be blocked when transaction or daily limits are breached."""

    def test_single_payment_exceeds_max_transaction_limit(
        self, bill_payment_service
    ):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidPaymentAmountError, PaymentLimitExceededError)):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("50000.01"))

    def test_daily_limit_exceeded_raises(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        authorised_customer,
        funded_account,
    ):
        """Customer has $100 daily limit; payment of $150 must be blocked."""
        authorised_customer.daily_payment_limit = Decimal("100.00")
        authorised_customer.daily_payments_so_far = Decimal("0.00")
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account
        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError) as exc_info:
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("150.00"))
        assert exc_info.value.limit == Decimal("100.00")

    def test_cumulative_payments_exceed_daily_limit(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        authorised_customer,
        funded_account,
    ):
        """Second payment tips customer over their daily limit."""
        authorised_customer.daily_payment_limit = Decimal("1000.00")
        authorised_customer.daily_payments_so_far = Decimal("900.00")
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account
        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("200.00"))

    def test_payment_at_exactly_daily_limit_is_accepted(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        authorised_customer,
        funded_account,
    ):
        authorised_customer.daily_payment_limit = Decimal("1000.00")
        authorised_customer.daily_payments_so_far = Decimal("900.00")
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("100.00"))
        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 6 – Settlement date: before cut-off
# ===========================================================================


class TestSettlementDateBeforeCutOff:
    """
    Payments submitted BEFORE the biller cut-off time must settle on
    the NEXT business day (T+1).
    """

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "initiated_at, expected_settlement",
        [
            # Monday 10:00 → Tuesday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 11),
            ),
            # Wednesday 14:59 → Thursday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 12, 14, 59, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 13),
            ),
            # Thursday 00:01 → Friday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 13, 0, 1, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 14),
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_settlement_is_next_business_day_before_cut_off(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        initiated_at,
        expected_settlement,
    ):
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == expected_settlement

    def test_friday_before_cutoff_settles_monday(self, bill_payment_service):
        """Friday before cut-off → skip weekend → Monday settlement."""
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 14, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)  # Friday
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == date(2024, 6, 17)  # Monday

    def test_settlement_skips_public_holiday(self, bill_payment_service):
        """Day before a public holiday before cut-off → T+2."""
        # Stub calendar to mark 2024-06-11 as a public holiday
        bill_payment_service._calendar_service.is_business_day.side_effect = (
            lambda d: d != date(2024, 6, 11)
        )
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == date(2024, 6, 12)


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 7 – Settlement date: at or after cut-off
# ===========================================================================


class TestSettlementDateAfterCutOff:
    """
    Payments submitted AT or AFTER the biller cut-off must settle on
    T+2 (next business day + 1).
    """

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "initiated_at, expected_settlement",
        [
            # Monday exactly at cut-off → Wednesday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 10, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 12),
            ),
            # Monday 15:01 → Wednesday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 10, 15, 1, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 12),
            ),
            # Monday 23:59 → Wednesday
            (
                datetime(2024, 6, 10, 23, 59, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ),
                date(2024, 6, 12),
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_settlement_is_t_plus_2_at_or_after_cut_off(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        initiated_at,
        expected_settlement,
    ):
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == expected_settlement

    def test_thursday_after_cutoff_settles_monday(self, bill_payment_service):
        """Thursday after cut-off → skip Friday and weekend for some billers
        If Friday is a business day: Friday + Monday → settlement Monday."""
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 13, 15, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)  # Thursday
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == date(2024, 6, 17)  # Monday

    def test_friday_after_cutoff_settles_tuesday(self, bill_payment_service):
        """Friday after cut-off → skip weekend → Tuesday settlement."""
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 14, 16, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)  # Friday
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date == date(2024, 6, 18)  # Tuesday

    def test_cut_off_boundary_is_inclusive(self, bill_payment_service):
        """15:00:00 exactly is treated as AT cut-off (same as after)."""
        before = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 14, 59, 59, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        at_cutoff = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)

        result_before = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=before)
        result_at = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=at_cutoff)

        assert result_before.settlement_date != result_at.settlement_date
        assert result_at.settlement_date > result_before.settlement_date


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 8 – Settlement date: determinism
# ===========================================================================


class TestSettlementDateDeterminism:
    """Same inputs must always produce the same settlement date."""

    def test_identical_inputs_produce_identical_settlement_date(
        self, bill_payment_service
    ):
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        results = [
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
            for _ in range(5)
        ]
        dates = {r.settlement_date for r in results}
        assert len(dates) == 1, "Settlement date must be deterministic"

    def test_settlement_date_does_not_depend_on_payment_amount(
        self, bill_payment_service
    ):
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        result_small = make_payment(
            bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("1.00"), initiated_at=initiated_at
        )
        result_large = make_payment(
            bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("50000.00"), initiated_at=initiated_at
        )
        assert result_small.settlement_date == result_large.settlement_date

    def test_settlement_date_is_never_in_the_past(self, bill_payment_service):
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date >= initiated_at.date()

    def test_settlement_date_is_a_business_day(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
    ):
        """Settlement date must never fall on a Saturday or Sunday."""
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        result = make_payment(bill_payment_service, initiated_at=initiated_at)
        assert result.settlement_date.weekday() < 5  # Mon–Fri


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 9 – Idempotency / duplicate detection
# ===========================================================================


class TestIdempotency:
    """Duplicate submissions with the same reference must not double-debit."""

    def test_duplicate_payment_reference_returns_original_result(
        self, bill_payment_service
    ):
        first = make_payment(
            bill_payment_service,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            reference="INV-DUP-001",
        )
        # Second call simulates resubmission
        bill_payment_service._payment_repo.find_by_reference.return_value = (
            BillPayment(
                payment_id=first.payment_id,
                status=PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED,
                amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            )
        )
        second = make_payment(
            bill_payment_service,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            reference="INV-DUP-001",
        )
        assert first.payment_id == second.payment_id


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 10 – Integration-style smoke test
# ===========================================================================


class TestEndToEndSmoke:
    """Lightweight end-to-end check using only real objects where feasible."""

    def test_full_successful_payment_flow(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        funded_account,
        registered_biller,
    ):
        initiated_at = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0, tzinfo=SYDNEY_TZ)
        result = make_payment(
            bill_payment_service,
            amount=Decimal("250.00"),
            reference="INV-E2E-001",
            initiated_at=initiated_at,
        )

        assert result.status == PaymentStatus.ACCEPTED
        assert result.amount == Decimal("250.00")
        assert result.biller_id == registered_biller.biller_id
        assert result.settlement_date == date(2024, 6, 11)
        assert result.payment_id is not None

    def test_full_rejection_insufficient_funds_flow(
        self,
        bill_payment_service,
        zero_balance_account,
    ):
        bill_payment_service._account_repo.get.return_value = zero_balance_account
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            make_payment(bill_payment_service, amount=Decimal("1.00"))
```

---

## Design Notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Layered from primitive domain objects up to the service, keeping tests independent |
| **Boundary values** | `$1.00`, `$0.99`, `$50,000.00`, `$50,000.01`, exact balance match — all covered |
| **Cut-off boundary** | `14:59:59` vs `15:00:00` tested explicitly for inclusive/exclusive semantics |
| **Determinism** | Same inputs called 5× to prove no hidden randomness |
| **Negative paths** | Separate classes for *insufficient funds*, *unauthorised*, *limits exceeded* — each with dedicated error assertions |
| **Calendar edge cases** | Friday → Monday, public holiday skip, Thursday-after-cut-off → Monday |
| **Error richness** | Exceptions carry typed attributes (`shortfall`, `available_balance`, `customer_id`) so callers can surface meaningful messages |
| **No production code assumed complete** | Mocks isolate the service from repo/gateway/calendar — tests fail for the right reason |

# pytest Tests for Bill Payment Requirements

```python
"""
Test Suite: Bill Payment - Registered Biller
Requirement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000
             with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.
             Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, time
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service interfaces assumed to exist in production code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from payments.services.bill_payment import BillPaymentService
from payments.models import (
    Account,
    BillPayment,
    Biller,
    Customer,
    PaymentResult,
    PaymentStatus,
    SettlementDate,
)
from payments.exceptions import (
    BillerNotRegisteredError,
    CustomerNotAuthorisedError,
    DailyLimitExceededError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    InvalidPaymentAmountError,
    PaymentLimitExceededError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MIN_PAYMENT = Decimal("1.00")
MAX_PAYMENT = Decimal("50000.00")
SYDNEY_TZ = ZoneInfo("Australia/Sydney")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def registered_biller() -> Biller:
    """A fully registered biller with a 3 PM AEST cut-off."""
    return Biller(
        biller_id="BILLER-001",
        name="City Water Authority",
        is_registered=True,
        cut_off_time=time(15, 0),          # 15:00 local
        cut_off_timezone=SYDNEY_TZ,
        settlement_lead_days=1,             # next business day aft

telemetry.raw_response_head:
# pytest Tests for Bill Payment Requirements

```python
"""
Test Suite: Bill Payment - Registered Biller
Requirement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000
             with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.
             Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, time
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service interfaces assumed to exist in production code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from payments.services.bill_payment import BillPaymentService
from payments.models import (
    Account,
    BillPayment,
    Biller,
    Customer,
    PaymentResult,
    PaymentStatus,
    SettlementDate,
)
from payments.exceptions import (
    BillerNotRegisteredError,
    CustomerNotAuthorisedError,
    DailyLimitExceededError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    InvalidPaymentAmountError,
    PaymentLimitExceededError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MIN_PAYMENT = Decimal("1.00")
MAX_PAYMENT = Decimal("50000.00")
SYDNEY_TZ = ZoneInfo("Australia/Sydney")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def registered_biller() -> Biller:
    """A fully registered biller with a 3 PM AEST cut-off."""
    return Biller(
        biller_id="BILLER-001",
        name="City Water Authority",
        is_registered=True,
        cut_off_time=time(15, 0),          # 15:00 local
        cut_off_timezone=SYDNEY_TZ,
        settlement_lead_days=1,             # next business day after cut-off
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def authorised_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-001",
        name="Jane Doe",
        is_active=True,
        is_authorised_for_bill_pay=True,
        daily_payment_limit=Decimal("100000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def unauthorised_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-002",
        name="John Blocked",
        is_active=True,
        is_authorised_for_bill_pay=False,
        daily_payment_limit=Decimal("100000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def funded_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-001",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
        available_balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def low_balance_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-002",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("0.50"),
        available_balance=Decimal("0.50"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def zero_balance_account(authorised_customer: Customer) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-003",
        owner=authorised_customer,
        balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        available_balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        currency="AUD",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def bill_payment_service(
    registered_biller: Biller,
    funded_account: Account,
) -> BillPaymentService:
    """Real service wired with mock repository/gateway collaborators."""
    payment_repo = MagicMock()
    account_repo = MagicMock()
    biller_registry = MagicMock()
    calendar_service = MagicMock()

    biller_registry.get.return_value = registered_biller
    account_repo.get.return_value = funded_account

    return BillPaymentService(

        account_repo=account_repo,
        biller_registry=biller_registry,
